races.race_name: Follow @attheraces On X Apprentice Handicap

runners.name: Balgowan
runners.spotlight: Has modest 1-28 strike-rate (includes five hurdle runs) but was denied a clear run before finishing well for close third over C&D last month and gets another chance off the same mark today; respected.
runners.comment: Has modest strike-rate but finished well for close third over C&D last month

runners.name: Sapphire Sirocco
runners.spotlight: Scored over C&D off basement mark for Pat Phelan in November; changed hands for 4,400gns after disappointing C&D run in January and makes stable debut after 110-day break but still worth a market check.
runners.comment: C&D winner for Pat Phelan in November; makes stable debut after 4,400gns sale

runners.name: Charles Morin
runners.spotlight: Yet to add to his 1m1f turf win here in 2024 but went close over 1m4f here (AW) in February and also ran well when fifth (just behind Balgowan) over C&D last month; probably won't be far away.
runners.comment: Went close over 1m4f here in February and last month's C&D fifth was also a good effort

runners.name: Regally Blonde
runners.spotlight: Dual turf winner (1m6f/2m) last summer; off since two below-par runs in the autumn, though, and still on fairly tough mark for this rare AW run.
runners.comment: Dual turf winner last summer; resumes on fairly tough mark; rarely seen on AW
runners.owner: Case Racing Partnership & Anita J Lush

runners.name: Adrian
runners.spotlight: Returns from break on career-low mark but this regressive 9yo is yet to add to his German Group 3 success in 2021 and is difficult to enthuse over.
runners.comment: Back from break on career-low mark but has not won since 2021; difficult to enthuse over

runners.name: Everest
runners.spotlight: Finished well for narrow win in 1m4f classified here last month, opening her account on 15th start under all codes; soundly beaten in Wolverhampton handicap over that trip since but could plausibly have untapped potential over 2m on the Flat.
runners.comment: Won 1m4f classified here last month and could plausibly have untapped potential over 2m
runners.owner: Alan Jamieson Site Services & G L Moore

runners.name: In The Post
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage 4yo; safely held in first three handicaps (1m-1m2f) but this big hike up in trip is an interesting move; cheekpieces removed after one use; well worth a glance in the bettting.
runners.comment: Safely held in three handicaps (1m-1m2f) but this hike up in trip is an interesting move

runners.name: Chillhi
runners.spotlight: Flat and dual hurdle winner during busy summer for James Owen last year but well beaten (over hurdles) on stable debut on Tuesday and returns to the Flat with quite a bit to prove.
runners.comment: Well beaten over hurdles on stable debut on Tuesday; back on Flat with fair bit to prove

runners.name: Laravie
runners.spotlight: Developed into fairly useful hurdler when fitted with a visor last year, winning four times (2m4f-2m7f); didn't show a great deal in three AW novice races (8.6f-1m3f) in March but this longer trip ought to suit much better and she has the visor refitted on her Flat handicap debut; must be considered.
runners.comment: Fairly useful hurdler; up in trip for Flat handicap debut; could be very dangerous

races.race_name: Amwins And Aegis Living The Dream Handicap

runners.name: Travel Agent
runners.spotlight: Came good for new stable at third attempt, overcoming some traffic issues before winning readily at Wolverhampton (6f) ten days ago; 4lb rise does not look excessive and this C&D also suits; good chance.
runners.comment: Won readily at Wolverhampton last week and this C&D also suits; good chance

runners.name: Diamond Dreamer
runners.spotlight: Didn't fire at Chelmsford last time but was a close second off a reduced mark here (5f) two starts ago and this return to 6f won't be a problem; can make his presence felt.
runners.comment: Didn't fire last time but was second off reduced mark here (5f) two runs ago

runners.name: Mart
runners.spotlight: Gained two of his seven wins over this C&D; below form lately but endured a wide trip when last in action and is now 3lb lower than when a close second over C&D in January.
runners.comment: Below form lately but now 3lb lower than when going close over C&D in January

runners.name: Maharajas Express
runners.spotlight: Won three times over 5f on AW/turf last year; stays 6f perfectly well and can easily be excused his defeat on heavy ground in September but has fitness to prove after a seven-month break.
runners.comment: Triple 5f winner in 2025; stays 6f perfectly well but has fitness to prove after layoff

runners.name: Lequinto
runners.spotlight: Into the veteran stage now and it's a long time since his latest AW win but he made the frame over C&D two starts ago and kept on well for second behind a next-time-out winner at Wolverhampton (6f) on his most recent outing.
runners.comment: Kept on for second behind next-time-out winner at Wolverhampton last month; considered

races.race_name: Free Race Replays On attheraces.com Restricted Maiden Fillies' Stakes (Bands C And D) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Barbuda Bay
runners.spotlight: Not gone on from last August's debut second here, falling well below market expectations on her comeback three weeks ago; said to have raced freely that day, upped to 1m, and will need to settle going further again.
runners.comment: Raced freely on comeback, upped to 1m, and will need to settle going further again

runners.name: Ticker Tape
runners.spotlight: Very well related and today's longer trip is likely to suit on pedigree, with a steadily run 8.6f never going to prove optimal on her Wolverhampton debut late last month; will only have learned from that.
runners.comment: Very well related and today's longer trip is likely to suit on pedigree; should improve

runners.name: Madrisa
runners.spotlight: First foal of an unraced half-sister to dual 1m2f Group 3 winner Extra Elusive and the yard's Group 2 Gimcrack winner Ajaya; blinkers go straight on for a stable yet to hit top gear for the season.
runners.comment: First foal of unraced mare; blinkers go straight on for a stable yet to hit top gear

runners.name: Lady Magu
runners.spotlight: 30,000gns yearling who's the third foal of a maiden and a sister to an ordinary Irish hurdler; been found a weak-looking maiden on debut and the market will guide.
runners.comment: 30,000gns yearling; been found a weak-looking maiden on debut and the market will guide

runners.name: Neswick
runners.spotlight: Seventh foal of a dual Tapeta winner over further; trainer, who also runs St Hilda, doesn't have the best of records with 3yos on the AW in recent times.
runners.comment: Seventh foal of a dual Tapeta winner over further; trainer also runs St Hilda

runners.name: Spinney
runners.spotlight: Dam useful at around this trip but she's produced nothing of note and this filly went for just 1,000gns as a yearling; perhaps best watched unless there's good money for her.
runners.comment: Went for just 1,000gns as yearling; perhaps best watched unless there's good money for her

runners.name: Billingsgate Gold
runners.spotlight: Huge prices for two 1m Kempton novices earlier in the year and ran as expected each time.
runners.comment: Huge prices for two 1m Kempton novices earlier in the year and ran as expected each time

runners.name: St Hilda
runners.spotlight: Needed the experience from a wide stall when shaping fairly on her 7f debut here last November (some way off the front three); entitled to improve and her dam won her maiden at around this trip.
runners.comment: Shaped fairly on 7f debut here last November; entitled to improve; dam won at this trip

runners.name: Love Fresh
runners.spotlight: Dam won over 2m on Tapeta (0-65 handicap) for the yard; easy to back, finished well held on her 1m debut five weeks ago and may well be one for handicaps down the line.
runners.comment: Easy to back, finished well held on her 1m debut; may be one for handicaps down the line

runners.name: West Hill Rosie
runners.spotlight: Pulled hard when tailed off in a bumper in February and again over 1m round Kempton early in the month; can't be backed.
runners.comment: Pulled hard when tailed off in a bumper in February and again over 1m early in the month

runners.name: Bel Air Flyer
runners.spotlight: Yard made a bright start but this filly offered little in two runs early last summer, pulling hard and finishing tailed off each time; low-grade handicaps are probably next up.
runners.comment: Little in two runs early last summer, pulling hard and finishing tailed off each time

runners.name: Last Flight
runners.spotlight: Not persevered with once her chance had gone in an ordinary fillies' novice at Newcastle late last year; drawn widest as she returns from four months off and goes up in trip.
runners.comment: Not persevered with on debut; drawn widest on her comeback as she goes up in trip

races.race_name: Tia Giggle Gallop Handicap

runners.name: Silver Trumpet
runners.spotlight: Found 1m stretching him on his comeback from five months off and is entitled to improve, back in the cheekpieces last sported last July; still paying the price for his easy Epsom win last August, though, and there's not much wiggle room off this mark.
runners.comment: Found 1m stretching him on his comeback; not much wiggle room off this mark

runners.name: Lunario
runners.spotlight: Last four wins have come in high summer, including two from the front last year for Jack Dace (rides Tiger Crusade), and he remains weighted to his best; didn't offer much on his comeback three weeks ago and an easy 7f is a bare minimum these days.
runners.comment: Last four wins have come in high summer; didn't offer much on his comeback three weeks ago

runners.name: Port Road
runners.spotlight: Wins both came off a 3lb lower mark over 1m round Kempton, latterly in January; there's nothing to suggest he's best caught fresh, so he looks to have a bit to overcome back from three months off.
runners.comment: Nothing to suggest he's best caught fresh; bit to overcome back from three months off

runners.name: Freddie's Star
runners.spotlight: Chelmsford win at long odds-on last November didn't amount to much and he's perhaps best judged on his subsequent handicap debut, when never involved; needs to improve a deal on that back from five months off.
runners.comment: Never involved on handicap debut when last seen; needs to improve a deal on that

runners.name: Tiger Crusade
runners.spotlight: Consistent record round here (2-4 over C&D) and is back on the mark off which he won here in January; not in the same form of late, there's always a chance he'll blow the start and it's no certainty the race will be set up for him.
runners.comment: Consistent record round here (2-4 over C&D); no certainty the race will be set up for him

runners.name: El Matador
runners.spotlight: Won two novices over this trip last May before finishing held in some decent-quality handicaps (among the favourites each time); not fully exposed as yet and this is a deal less competitive on his AW debut, back from seven months off, having been gelded.
runners.comment: Not fully exposed yet and this is a deal less competitive on his AW debut; leading claims

runners.name: King's Vanity
runners.spotlight: Only seen once since leaving Andrew Balding 18 months ago, when well held last October; won his novice over C&D and the hood going back on should help but it's hard to know whether he's still up to this sort of mark.
runners.comment: Only seen once since leaving Andrew Balding 18 months ago; plenty to prove

races.race_name: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 Handicap

runners.name: Gladiadora
runners.spotlight: Has done most racing over further and an easy 7f is a bare minimum but she's returned from a short break with two solid efforts under Liam Wright; up another 2lb for her Nottingham near-miss but has to enter calculations.
runners.comment: Nudged up another 2lb for her Nottingham near-miss but has to enter calculations
runners.owner: Mrs R Coleman And G Glendining

runners.name: Vitalline
runners.spotlight: Serial slow starter who switches back from blinkers to the visor sported for last month's 0-50 Kempton win; will be happier back up from 6f but is yet another who'll need some luck from a wide stall.
runners.comment: Will be happier back up from 6f but is yet another who'll need some luck from a wide stall

runners.name: Miss Moonshine
runners.spotlight: Wildly inconsistent mare whose C&D dead-heat in February (third course win) came from out of the blue at 33-1; reverted to type since and it's hard to know what to expect as always.
runners.comment: Wildly inconsistent mare; hard to know what to expect as always

runners.name: Francisco
runners.spotlight: Often misses the break and can pull hard, so will always come with risks attached; has resumed in good form, though, latterly behind a couple of subsequent winners at Chelmsford, and is quite capable off this mark if things fall his way.
runners.comment: Often misses the break and can pull hard but capable off this mark if things fall his way

runners.name: Twirler
runners.spotlight: Goes well round switchback tracks and is back down in class today having contested a couple of 0-70 handicaps over C&D latterly; not much room for manoeuvre off this mark but is capable of running well.
runners.comment: Goes well at switchback tracks; not much room for manoeuvre off this mark but is capable

runners.name: Dayman
runners.spotlight: Regressive gelding who's often been inclined to pull hard since returning from a year off; winless since his 3yo days and there are safer options.
runners.comment: Inclined to pull hard since returning from a year off; winless since his 3yo days

runners.name: Bint Havana Gold
runners.spotlight: Dual 7f winner early last year but has done a lot of recent racing in sprints and she pulled hard back up to this trip last month; a danger that she'll do the same again from the outside stall and much will hinge on how she settles early.
runners.comment: Pulled hard back up to this trip latest; much will hinge on how she settles from stall 11
runners.owner: Alan & Christine Bright And John Henwood

runners.name: Cooramook
runners.spotlight: Remains a maiden and has had plenty of chances but she went close a couple of times round here for Harry Eustace; better effort at a big price last time, switched from cheekpieces to blinkers, and holds claims on that.
runners.comment: Went close a couple of times round here for Harry Eustace; one to consider

runners.name: Lunanova
runners.spotlight: Won three over 1m at Brighton last summer, latterly off a 1lb lower mark, before losing her way; a poor comeback effort for her new yard four weeks ago leaves her hard to recommend.
runners.comment: Poor comeback effort for her new yard four weeks ago leaves her hard to recommend

runners.name: Twitch
runners.spotlight: On the downgrade last year; third to Vitalline in a 0-50 classified event on his Kempton comeback early last month but has since run only fairly from the front over C&D; others appeal more.
runners.comment: On the downgrade; only ran fairly from the front over C&D latest; others appeal more
runners.owner: The Limes Of Epsom Racing Club

runners.name: Victory Sound
runners.spotlight: String of poor efforts for two yards last year and two more this spring, latterly over C&D; this is easier again, dropped another 5lb in second-time cheekpieces, but he's hard to make a case for.
runners.comment: String of poor efforts for two yards last year and two more this spring

races.race_name: Follow @attheraces On Instagram Handicap

runners.name: Caviar Cowboy
runners.spotlight: Progressive colt who posted a very good second in 6f novice here last month, finishing well having conceded first run to the winner (gone in again since); looks to have more to offer now handicapping back up in trip; big shout.
runners.comment: Progressive colt with more to offer on handicap debut back up in trip; big shout

runners.name: Mighty Vega
runners.spotlight: In good form for his current yard, scoring over C&D in March and runner-up back over C&D 13 days ago; well in the mix despite a 2lb rise.
runners.comment: C&D winner before runner-up back over C&D 13 days ago; up 2lb but he's well in the mix

runners.name: Tactical Blitz
runners.spotlight: Made a winning start in handicaps at Newcastle (6f) in March; backed it up with a solid third of seven at Wolverhampton (7f; cheekpieces first time) 17 days ago; not out of things.
runners.comment: Won at Newcastle and followed it with solid Wolverhampton third; not out of things

runners.name: Sagremor
runners.spotlight: Fair juvenile maiden; gelded/off five months and not disgraced given he went freely when eighth of 12 in 6f handicap at Southwell 20 days ago; needs to build on it.
runners.comment: Not disgraced when eighth in 6f handicap at Southwell 20 days ago; needs to build on it

runners.name: King Of Chaos
runners.spotlight: Got off the mark in a C&D maiden in February but has failed to kick on back handicapping, though not totally disgraced when fifth at Beverley (7.5f) 12 days ago; needs to step forward.
runners.comment: Off the mark in a C&D maiden but has failed to kick on back handicapping; more required

runners.name: Kilkenny Warrior
runners.spotlight: Ended 2025 with nursery wins at Southwell (6f) and Lingfield (7f); shaped as if the run was needed after five months off when seventh at Kempton (7f) last month and can get back on track here.
runners.comment: Fading seventh on return at Kempton (7f) last month; can get back on track here

runners.name: Storm Point
runners.spotlight: Off four months/gelded before taking a major step forward on his handicap debut when scoring at Kempton (7f) 26 days ago; up 4lb but the runner-up has gone in since; merits serious consideration with better still to come.
runners.comment: Major step forward when scoring at Kempton latest; merits serious consideration

runners.name: Pretty Ambitious
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in a pair of novices at Doncaster (7f) and Chelmsford (1m) before beating just one home in 7f Chelmsford maiden on her final run for Alice Haynes; it would come as no surprise to see this Wootton Bassett filly bounce back on yard/handicap debut.
runners.comment: Twice second for Alice Haynes at 2yrs; not ruled out on her yard/handicap debut

runners.name: If I Could Dream
runners.spotlight: Proved consistent as a 2yo without winning, signing off with a solid 7f nursery third at Haydock in October; off six months ahead of her Polytrack debut.
runners.comment: Consistent as a 2yo without winning; off six months ahead of her Polytrack debut

runners.name: Robert Anstruther
runners.spotlight: Failed to build on earlier promise when seventh on his handicap debut at Newbury (7f) in September; since gelded and could still do better on his seasonal return with his yard going well.
runners.comment: Seventh on Newbury handicap debut; since gelded and could still do better for in-form yard

runners.name: Havana Smile
runners.spotlight: Showed fair form in four runs at 2yrs, signing off with a good fourth in 7f Epsom nursery in September; since gelded and can't be discounted on his seasonal return.
runners.comment: Good fourth in 7f Epsom nursery in September; since gelded and not discounted on return

races.race_name: Download The At The Races App Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Son Of Man
runners.spotlight: Gained a first success (since his debut in 2023) when edging out Wonder by 1l in 15-runner handicap over C&D 24 days ago; 2lb worse off here but still not taken lightly.
runners.comment: First success since his debut over C&D 24 days ago; back up 4lb but not taken lightly

runners.name: Wonder
runners.spotlight: Thriving since the cheekpieces went on, scoring at Southwell (1m4f) in February and bettering that form when beaten 1l by Son Of Man over C&D 24 days ago; enjoys a 2lb weights pull now so he's a likely player.
runners.comment: Thriving since cheekpieces fitted; a very good second over C&D latest so a likely player

runners.name: Adjuvant
runners.spotlight: Winless since May 2023 but he reappeared from five months off the track with a good third of five in 2m Southwell handicap 18 days ago; not out of things on his Polytrack debut eased 1lb.
runners.comment: Winless since May 2023 but good Southwell third on return; not out of things

runners.name: Skibo
runners.spotlight: Shaped well when placed in a pair of 1m2f Salisbury handicaps last summer; off eight months but this low-mileage son of New Approach should have more to offer in 2026, especially over 1m4f; interesting.
runners.comment: Shaped well last summer; should have more to offer at 1m4f; not without interest on return

runners.name: Way Of Life
runners.spotlight: Kickstarted 2026 with success at Wolverhampton (9.5f) in March and backed it up with a good third of 12 in another 9.5f handicap there since; this C&D scorer can go well again off the same mark.
runners.comment: Won at Wolverhampton before good third there since; this C&D scorer can go well again

runners.name: Metallo
runners.spotlight: On a long losing run but he comes here in decent form for his current yard, sixth of 14 in 1m2f handicap here 24 days ago; possibilities on his first go at this trip.
runners.comment: On a long losing run but he comes here in decent form for his current yard; possibilities

runners.name: Rogue Impact
runners.spotlight: Sole victory came over C&D but he has failed to beat a rival on his last two runs, gelded before coming in last of 9 at Kempton (1m4f) on his return three weeks ago; hard to warm to.
runners.comment: Sole victory came over C&D but he has failed to beat a rival on his last two runs

runners.name: Youthful King
runners.spotlight: C&D winner but he ended 2025 below par and beat one on his return from six months off in 1m4f Kempton handicap three weeks ago; needs to take a major step forward.
runners.comment: C&D winner but he beat one on his return at Kempton three weeks ago though

races.race_name: Download The Fairplay App Apprentice Handicap

runners.name: Bohemian Breeze
runners.spotlight: Showed clear signs of a return to form with fourth-place finish in latest AW start and he is just as effective on turf; possibilities off 1lb below last winning mark.
runners.comment: Clear signs of a return to form last time; possibilities off 1lb below last winning mark

runners.name: Itsgottobefun
runners.spotlight: Belied his big odds with a placed effort on penultimate outing (AW) but otherwise has weak claims on form.
runners.comment: Ran well on penultimate start but otherwise holds weak claims on form
runners.owner: Mrs Wendy Jarrett & J S Moore

runners.name: Marinakis
runners.spotlight: Gained all three wins for Ian Williams at classified level on AW; remains to be seen whether he can hold his form back on turf; wears new headgear on stable debut.
runners.comment: Remains to be seen whether he can hold his form back on turf; stable debut

runners.name: Endofastorm
runners.spotlight: Dual winner at Bath, including off 1lb higher in most recent turf attempt; not in the same form on AW since but may prove resurgent returned to this course; one to consider.
runners.comment: Dual winner at Bath, including off 1lb higher in most recent turf attempt; one to consider

runners.name: Knightmare
runners.spotlight: Still a maiden and has inconsistent form for current yard; campaigned mostly on AW and has posted two duck eggs on turf; enough to prove upped further in trip.
runners.comment: Maiden who has enough to prove back on turf and upped further in trip

runners.name: Moon Over The Sea
runners.spotlight: Only 1-14 on turf but gained the win at Bath and returns here in form, having been placed in last two AW attempts (seven-time winner in that sphere); shortlisted.
runners.comment: Only 1-14 on turf but gained the win at Bath; placed in last two AW starts; player

runners.name: Qaaeadd
runners.spotlight: Inconsistent maiden but posted one of his better efforts last time (9.5f, AW; second start for new yard) while shaping as if this new trip is worth exploring.
runners.comment: Posted one of his better efforts last time and this new trip is worth exploring

runners.name: My O My
runners.spotlight: Seven-race maiden, campaigned mostly on AW; not particularly solid on form; returns to turf for first time since debut.
runners.comment: Not particularly solid on form

races.race_name: Silvershine Turf Services Handicap

runners.name: Party Island
runners.spotlight: Has a better strike-rate on AW than on turf but he's in form, creditable third at Lingfield on Good Friday last time; gained last win off 5lb higher (in July 2024); shortlisted.
runners.comment: In form on AW; gained last win off 5lb higher; possibilities

runners.name: Bobby Dassler
runners.spotlight: Has a modest strike-rate but his Bath record is largely sound and includes a win last October; soundly beaten here on stable/seasonal debut, albeit perhaps needed the run.
runners.comment: Soundly beaten here on stable/seasonal debut

runners.name: Thapa Vc
runners.spotlight: 13-time winner who has frame possibilities returned to Bath with AW reappearance under his belt; record here comprises a 2l fourth in this race last year and two wins over 1m.
runners.comment: Bath record comprises a 2l fourth in this race last year and two wins over 1m

runners.name: Rating
runners.spotlight: AW winner for current yard (maiden previously); ran respectably (1m, good to soft) on seasonal debut, taking Bath record to 333; has form over 1m2f; looks a solid contender.
runners.comment: Reappearance effort took his Bath record to 333; solid

runners.name: Paps Of Jura
runners.spotlight: Seven-race maiden who last two efforts (8.5f/1m4f, AW) suggest this return to 1m2f, combined with first-time hood, may prove ideal; improvement is not out of the question.
runners.comment: May improve returned to 1m2f with hood added

runners.name: Moby Quick
runners.spotlight: Not particularly solid on his AW form (at up to 9.5f) for current stable; gained sole win on turf (6f; off 3lb higher) but has something to prove over this new trip.
runners.comment: Has something to prove over this new trip

runners.name: Raintown
runners.spotlight: Has a better strike-rate on AW than on turf and returns to this sphere with a doubt over his current form; however, may prove resurgent off current mark dropped in class.
runners.comment: May prove resurgent off current mark dropped in class
runners.owner: Ms Caroline Instone & J S Moore

races.race_name: Silvershine Turf Services Support Bath Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Global Success
runners.spotlight: Belied her 50-1 odds and showed some promise with fifth of 13 at Bath (5f, good) ten days ago, shaping as if today's extra yardage will suit; sets only an ordinary standard but may show improvement.
runners.comment: Showed promise with fifth of 13 at Bath; sets the form standard

runners.name: Leucothea
runners.spotlight: April foal; 52,000gns yearling; Soldier's Call half-sister to 5f 2yo winner Wateen (RPR 83); unraced dam out of 7f/1m 2yo Listed scorer, is granddaughter of 5f 2yo Group 3 winner; likely type on breeding.
runners.comment: 52,000gns yearling; has a precocious pedigree; likely type

runners.name: Crown Of Ivy
runners.spotlight: February foal; 60,000euros foal, 50,000euros yearling; from the first crop of Naval Crown; half-brother to 6f winner King Of Bears (including 2yo; RPR 92); yard has had a good record with 2yos down the years.
runners.comment: 50,000gns yearling; by Naval Crown; yard has a good record with 2yos

runners.name: Dandyman Dan
runners.spotlight: April foal; 38,000gns yearling; Dandy Man half-brother to Italian 7f winner Iraklion (including 2yo); dam unraced, out of French 6f 2yo Group 1 scorer; one of two newcomers for his stable; market instructive.
runners.comment: 38,000gns yearling; by Dandy Man; one of two newcomers for his yard
runners.owner: Eric Griffiths, Mrs T Burns & Partner

runners.name: King Of Montenegro
runners.spotlight: April foal; £25,000 yearling; Earthlight half-brother to 6f winner Sacred Falls (RPR 78); dam 6f/7f 2yo scorer (85), half-sister to Breeders' Cup Turf Sprint winner Belvoir Bay; check the betting.
runners.comment: £25,000 yearling; by Earthlight out of 6f/7f 2yo scorer

runners.name: Sovereign Glory
runners.spotlight: February foal; 34,000gns yearling; by Cotai Glory and first foal of 1m-winning (RPR 81) half-sister to 7f Group 2 scorer Sacred; heed the market signals.
runners.comment: 34,000gns yearling; by Cotai Glory and first foal of 1m winner

runners.name: Undercover Affair
runners.spotlight: March foal; by Havana Grey; sibling to four winners, notably Rosy Affair (5f/6f including Group 3; RPR 107); dam 5f 2yo scorer (82); one of two newcomers for his yard; market informative.
runners.comment: Son of Havana Grey; one of two newcomers for his stable

runners.name: Downloadmylife
runners.spotlight: Always behind in the Brocklesby, having been a 40-1 shot; doesn't seem an imminent winner and is perhaps best watched unless the market signals are stronger this time.
runners.comment: Always behind in the Brocklesby

races.race_name: Mark Futcher Half-Century Handicap

runners.name: Alvin
runners.spotlight: Campaigned on AW this year, best form when scoring readily over 5f in February and close third (despite wide trip) over 6f last time; may do better still; possibilities back on turf.
runners.comment: AW form this year includes a win in February and good third last time; may do better still

runners.name: Solar Invincible
runners.spotlight: Successful over C&D (good; switched back to turf from AW) last time, taking record since wearing cheekpieces to 21341 and form figures in this sphere to 441; upped in class but receives weight all round; respected with further progress plausible.
runners.comment: Scored over C&D last time, taking record since wearing cheekpieces to 21341; respected

runners.name: Comic Strip
runners.spotlight: Has built up a solid record on AW, latterly 1123 since handicapping and dropped to 5f; good effort on reappearance last time; likely player, assuming he proves as good on turf.
runners.comment: Has built up a solid record on AW; likely player, assuming he's as effective on turf

runners.name: London Is Blue
runners.spotlight: Ended last season with a poor effort on AW; progressive (211) in handicaps on turf, the wins over 5.3f at Brighton, and may have more to offer in this sphere; still of interest.
runners.comment: Progressive (211) in handicaps on turf; possibilities returned to this sphere

runners.name: Guernsey Angel
runners.spotlight: Campaigned on AW since handicapping, winning twice as a 2yo but showing no further progress this year; chance partly depends on the effects of this return to turf.
runners.comment: Campaigned on AW since handicapping; no further progress this year

runners.name: Saucy Jane
runners.spotlight: Consistent efforts in AW sprints this year and ties in with Comic Strip on the latest one; registered sole success on turf and looks a solid contender returned to this sphere.
runners.comment: Consistent on AW this year; registered sole success on turf; solid contender
runners.owner: Nick Bradley Racing 16 And Jmr Partners

runners.name: Angel Numbers
runners.spotlight: Best effort since switched to AW when runner-up on penultimate start; capable of being involved and she gained sole win on turf; however, 0-8 since that debut performance.
runners.comment: Capable of being involved back on turf but is 0-8 since debut success

runners.name: Alkuwarrior
runners.spotlight: Maiden winner for previous yard; absent and gelded since failing to beat a rival last August on debut for new stable; best watched unless the market speaks positively.
runners.comment: Absent and gelded since failing to beat a rival last August on stable debut

runners.name: Duskaura
runners.spotlight: Not particularly solid on her AW form this term for new yard but may prove resurgent in this scenario, being a turf winner who is having first handicap attempt in this sphere.
runners.comment: Not solid on AW form this term for new yard but may fare better back on turf

runners.name: Artista
runners.spotlight: Steadily back to form in AW contests this year, creditable fourth latest; possibly on her way to a new peak and looks interesting back on turf; won in this sphere last spring.
runners.comment: Steadily back to form on AW this year; possibly on her way to a new peak; interesting

races.race_name: Bet Now With fairplaybet.co.uk Handicap

runners.name: The Feminine Urge
runners.spotlight: Successful three times for previous yard, most recently last June; creditable third over C&D (good) on stable/seasonal debut; may build on that effort; respected off the same mark.
runners.comment: Creditable third over C&D on stable/seasonal debut; may build on that effort; respected

runners.name: So Smart
runners.spotlight: Front-runner who is a five-time winner, including at Bath; respectable fourth over C&D on reappearance and ties in with new stablemate The Feminine Urge on that effort; shortlisted.
runners.comment: Ties in with new stablemate on C&D reappearance form; one for the shortlist

runners.name: Diamondsinthesand
runners.spotlight: Ties in with a couple of today's rivals on C&D reappearance form but ideally needs a return to further; yet to score over a bare 5f; gained his Bath win over the extended 5f.
runners.comment: Ideally needs a return to further; gained his Bath win over the extended 5f

runners.name: Toolatetonegotiate
runners.spotlight: Three-time winner on AW, most recently in January; 0-9 on turf and not helped by being 8lb higher than last attempt in this sphere; however, her consistency is a positive.
runners.comment: 0-9 on turf and on a rather difficult mark but her consistency is a positive

runners.name: The Thames Boatman
runners.spotlight: Multiple AW winner; hindered by being hampered on Good Friday last time; 0-15 on turf but ran creditably (finished second) in this race last year; place claims off same mark.
runners.comment: Multiple AW wins; 0-15 on turf but ran creditably (finished second) in this race last year

runners.name: Moe's Legacy
runners.spotlight: Has plenty of Bath form but mostly over the extended 5f, including three wins; below par returned to this venue last time after two sound AW performances at Wolverhampton.
runners.comment: Has plenty of Bath form but mostly over the extended 5f, including three wins

runners.name: Solar Edge
runners.spotlight: Has done his winning off marks in the 60s; mixed record (some good results, some not so good) off marks in the 70s and he lacks recent match practice; not the percentage call.
runners.comment: Not the percentage call off his current mark on return from layoff

races.race_name: Signs Express Handicap

runners.name: Eupator
runners.spotlight: AW form since wearing blinkers features his only two wins; 0-4 on turf and has a bit to prove with the headgear removed back in this sphere.
runners.comment: 0-4 on turf and has a bit to prove with headgear removed back in this sphere
runners.owner: Middleham Park Racing I and Partner

runners.name: Candonomore
runners.spotlight: Four wins at 1m and 7f for previous yard; 0-5 for current stable but he's threatening to regain the winning thread, beaten a short head at Yarmouth (7f, good to firm) last week when switched back to turf from AW; major contender off the same mark.
runners.comment: 0-5 for current stable but went close at Yarmouth last week returned to turf; big player

runners.name: Happy Banner
runners.spotlight: AW form features his sole win, as a 2yo, and close second at Chelmsford when last seen; by mo means certain to go one better, with turf record (76537) not totally convincing.
runners.comment: Seems ideally suited by AW; turf record (76537) isn't totally convincing

runners.name: Nakaaha
runners.spotlight: Completed a double last summer (maiden previously); not in the same form since but perhaps needed her Doncaster reappearance; may prove resurgent with that outing under her belt.
runners.comment: May prove resurgent with Doncaster reappearance under her belt

runners.name: Special Ghaiyyath
runners.spotlight: One win apiece for his two previous yards, taking his turf record to 22411; ran well on AW last time and sold for 25,000gns since; interesting returned to this sphere on debut for another new stable.
runners.comment: Turf record reads 22411; interesting back in this sphere on debut for another new yard

runners.name: Caledonian
runners.spotlight: Made a winning handicap debut in AW contest as a 2yo but failed to transfer the form back to turf last year; admittedly still has low mileage and may have a better 2026 campaign; heed the market signals.
runners.comment: Failed to transfer the form of his 2yo AW win back to turf last year

runners.name: Aspull
runners.spotlight: Raced four times in his first season last year, producing a solid novice record (132) on turf prior to a lesser effort in AW handicap; gelded since; remains open to further progress back in this sphere and reappearance may be the time to catch him; respected.
runners.comment: Remains open to further progress back on turf; has a solid novice record in this sphere

races.race_name: Signs Express Proudly Support Bath Racecourse Handicap

runners.name: Zoustellar
runners.spotlight: Came from off the pace to open account in first-time cheekpieces at Yarmouth (1m, good) this month, her seventh start; effectively 5lb higher here if Jack Callan's reduced claim is considered but might be seen to even better effect on this stiffer track.
runners.comment: Came from off the pace to open account in first-time cheekpieces at Yarmouth this month

runners.name: Arvana Belle
runners.spotlight: Scored on nursery debut at Brighton (6f, good) in August and finished close up there (6f/7f) on next two outings; upped in trip again for this reappearance outing; won't be far away if fully tuned out.
runners.comment: Ended last season in good form in Brighton nurseries; upped in trip for reappearance

runners.name: Denby's Dream
runners.spotlight: A maiden after seven starts but encountered some traffic before running on well to snatch second (no threat to winner) at Lingfield (1m, AW) a fortnight ago and remains unexposed over this trip; one to consider.
runners.comment: Ran on well for second at Lingfield this month and remains unexposed over 1m

runners.name: Miss Mambo
runners.spotlight: Gave pretty good account when third of six in C&D (good to soft) maiden won comfortably by short-odds favourite ten days ago, her fourth start, and could have some improvement to come in handicaps.
runners.comment: Ran well in recent C&D maiden and could have some improvement to come in handicaps

runners.name: Takeitorleaveit
runners.spotlight: Displayed improvement to reward strong market support over C&D (soft; first-time cheekpieces) on nursery debut in September; off since a lesser effort in October, though, and needs to prove himself on today's much faster ground.
runners.comment: Won soft-ground C&D nursery in September; off since lesser effort in October

runners.name: Mali Star
runners.spotlight: Just a respectable fifth on nursery debut at Southwell (1m, AW) in October and below that form at Chelmsford the following month; gelded during the winter, though, and improvement is possible this year.
runners.comment: Unplaced all five 2yo starts but improvement is possible this year

runners.name: Beryl's Girl
runners.spotlight: Ended three-race 2yo campaign with quite promising third in C&D (good to soft) maiden in September, albeit in a slowly run race; makes handicap debut after 226-day absence but has potential.
runners.comment: Ended three-race 2yo campaign with third in C&D maiden; makes handicap debut today

races.race_name: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)

runners.name: Victorious
runners.spotlight: Fourth foal; sister to 7f AW winner Stellenbosch (RPR 85), half-sister to winners Sugar Island (7f/1m 2yo inc Group 3; RPR 103) and Mother Nature (11.3f; 101); dam 11.3f winner (90), sister to winners Minding (1,000 Guineas/Oaks) and Tuesday (Oaks).
runners.comment: Sister to 7f AW winner and half-sister to Sugar Island (7f/1m 2yo inc Group 3; RPR 103)

runners.name: Aedhmar
runners.spotlight: Daughter of Blue Point is a sibling of many winners, including some smart juveniles; 2-1 fav when third of 6 at Cork, beaten only a neck in total on testing ground.
runners.comment: Sent off 2-1 fav when third of 6 at Cork, beaten only a neck in total on testing ground

runners.name: Ballinea Star
runners.spotlight: Made 26,000euros as a yearling; fourth foal; half-sister to 12.5f/14.3f winner Gangster Granny (RPR 80); dam Group-placed 6f 2yo winner (101), half-sister to Listed-placed French/Italian 7.5f-1m4f winner Jack Boy, out of unraced half-sister to 1m Group 1 winner Rajeem; missed last month's intended debut at Curragh (coughing); that Egan doesn't ride is not a positive sign.
runners.comment: Made 26,000euros as yearling; half-sister to winning stayer; dam Group-placed 2yo winner

runners.name: Beibhinn
runners.spotlight: First foal; dam placed 7f (inc AW 2yo; RPR 81), half-sister to winners Hispanic (useful 5f 2yo/6f, by No Nay Never), Ghaziyah (1m) and Mamluk (French 1m4f), out of 8.5f winning half-sister to French 2,000 Guineas winner Aussie Rules; yard red-hot and first 2yo runner hit the target lately.
runners.comment: First foal; dam placed 7f (inc AW 2yo; RPR 81) and is half-sister to several winners

runners.name: Livenka
runners.spotlight: Eighth foal; closely related to 1m2f-12.4f winner Derevo (inc AW/KSA; RPR 106), half-sister to five winners including Babouche (6f inc 2yo Group 1; 117), Zarinsk (6f-1m inc Group 2; 115) and Society Lion (6f-1m inc 2yo/AW; 102); those three all won first-time out for this yard; dam 7f/1m (Listed) winner (103).
runners.comment: Half-sister to five winners including yard's Phoenix Stakes winner Babouche

runners.name: Roeillaun
runners.spotlight: Bought for 30,000euros as a yearling; twelfth foal; closely related to 7f winner Risk Taker, (RPR 85), half-sister to winners Western Australia (7f 2yo/1m5f Listed; 112), Hoarding (7f-1m2f inc 2yo/Listed; 101) and Strada Facendo (7f; 86); dam 7f winner (75).
runners.comment: Bought for 30,000euros as a yearling; related to plenty of useful winners

races.race_name: Enjoy Racing TV With A FREE Trial Now Handicap

runners.name: One Cool Bear
runners.spotlight: Has twice gone close in 6f handicaps at Dundalk; fourth when favourite there last time; up in grade now, so no easy task; headgear applied for the first time.
runners.comment: Has twice gone close in 6f handicaps at Dundalk; fourth when favourite there last time

runners.name: Allsortz
runners.spotlight: Fair efforts on turf last term; won 6f AW maiden on second run back and that form was franked last week; mention of July Sales afterwards wouldn't be hugely encouraging, but perhaps it would be wrong to read too much into that.
runners.comment: Won 6f AW maiden on second run back and form was franked last last week

runners.name: This Guy
runners.spotlight: Maiden was runner-up three times in a row at Dundalk over the winter; not as good in recent AW starts; had ok form on fast turf when trained in Britain last term.
runners.comment: Runner-up three times in a row at Dundalk over the winter; not as good in recent AW starts

runners.name: Amiata
runners.spotlight: AW maiden winner over 6f in December; beaten just under 5l in Cork 5f rated contest; handicap debut.
runners.comment: AW maiden winner over 6f; beaten just under 5l in Cork 5f rated contest; handicap debut.

runners.name: Joyful Tidings
runners.spotlight: Beat smart performer when winning 5f maiden on debut last term and ran well behind recent Greenham winner in sales' contest next time; held in handicaps thus far; fitter for Cork comeback having been gelded last autumn, but Carroll prefers stablemate.
runners.comment: Held in handicaps thus far, fitter for Cork comeback having been gelded last autumn

runners.name: All The Girls
runners.spotlight: Looked exciting when winning AW maiden over 7f but, in retrospect, didn't beat a great deal; bumped into a smart horse next time; well held both starts this year, beaten 9l in Madrid Handicap on latest; drops to 6f and better ground a help, as might be cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Beaten 9l in Madrid Handicap on latest; drops to 6f and better ground a help
runners.owner: Elaine Shevlin & Cormac Farrell & Ms Denise McCourt

runners.name: Bad Boy Rizz
runners.spotlight: Consistent in sprint maidens last year; off the mark over 5f when last seen at Navan in October; ground was soft then, but goes on good ground too; handicap debut.
runners.comment: Consistent in sprint maidens last year; off the mark over 5f at Navan in October

runners.name: Wearing Stars
runners.spotlight: Twice placed over 6f on AW late last year; should come on from reappearance fourth at Cork (5f, soft); handicap debut.
runners.comment: Twice placed over 6f on AW; should come on from reappearance fourth at Cork (5f, soft)

runners.name: Coincidental Glory
runners.spotlight: Consistent but exposed compared to most of these; 7f AW nursery winner on final start last term and second in 1m claimer at Gowran on return; has placed form at this trip but probably vulnerable at it.
runners.comment: Consistent but exposed compared to most of these; second in 1m claimer at Gowran on return
runners.owner: Total Recall Racing Club & J Fairhurst & Cyril McMullan

runners.name: Treasured Royal
runners.spotlight: Maiden winner over this sort of trip at Navan; ran okay in AW Listed contest over 5f, not so good over 6f at Newmarket when last seen in late October; sights lowered here on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Maiden winner over this sort of trip; held in Listed contests since; sights lowered

runners.name: Whistling Jamesie
runners.spotlight: Won at 20-1 on handicap debut over 6f at Dundalk in October; down the field on last month's return over 7f at same course, in race won by stablemate; coming back to 6f looks the right move.
runners.comment: Won at 20-1 on handicap debut over 6f at Dundalk; down the field over 7f at same course

runners.name: Fontaine Khaled
runners.spotlight: Nice run in a good-ground Curragh maiden last year; well held both handicap starts lately, but a hint of promise under quiet handling last time and yard won this two years ago; wouldn't be too quick to dismiss her.
runners.comment: Hint of promise under quiet handling last time and yard won this two years ago
runners.owner: Dan Kiely & C O'Neill & Apollo Thoroughbreds

races.race_name: Colm White Bookmaker Handicap

runners.name: Indigo Dream
runners.spotlight: 0-6 on turf but in fine form on AW; won 7f Dundalk maiden in December and creditable efforts in valuable 7f Newcastle handicaps the last twice; drop to 6f a query but can't be discounted.
runners.comment: 0-6 on turf, in good form on the AW over 7f, drop to 6f a query but can't discount

runners.name: Bishopton
runners.spotlight: Resumed progress on seasonal return when a 0.5l winner at Dundalk last month; record of 1-10 on turf, the win coming in a 7f handicap at Down Royal; drop back to 6f a bit of a worry here; cheekpieces go on.
runners.comment: All three career wins at 7f inc' at Dundalk latest, drop to 6f a worry; cheekpieces on
runners.owner: Nigel O'Hare/D W Sloan/Milena C Mocchetti/Kabin Racing Synd

runners.name: Heavenly Power
runners.spotlight: One of three for yard; five-time winner, the most recent over C&D (yielding) in October 2024 off 4lb higher; shaped as though in need of the run last time at the Curragh and should be much sharper now; interesting.
runners.comment: Five-time winner including over C&D off 4lb higher, sharper for latest, interesting
runners.owner: Exors of the Late Mrs S Power

runners.name: Valiant Force
runners.spotlight: Sole win on turf in the 2023 Norfolk Stakes; has won five conditions races at Dundalk since, the most recent a 4.25l win over 5f in February; well beaten at Lingfield and Newcastle the last twice and others arrive in better form.
runners.comment: Best recent form on AW, well beaten last twice in Britain, others arrive in better form
runners.owner: AMO Racing Limited & Giselle De Aguiar

runners.name: Staysound Susie
runners.spotlight: Career-best on latest to land a nice pot at the Curragh (6f, sft-hvy) under this rider, her fourth win; ground-versatile but she'll need to improve again to follow up off a career-high mark of 90.
runners.comment: Career best to win on latest at the Curragh, needs to improve again off 7lb higher

runners.name: Howd'yadoit
runners.spotlight: 3yo; two wins last year included the Ballyhane Stakes over this C&D (gd-yld); came up short in Listed company subsequently and has been gelded; probably best watched on handicap debut and seasonal return.
runners.comment: 3yo, won the Ballyhane here last year, gelded, likely best watched in first h'cap

runners.name: Tango Flare
runners.spotlight: Finished last season in excellent form with a 50-1 win in the Bold Lad at the Curragh (6f, yielding) and a 1.75l second in a Listed race here (6f, yielding); off since October and has needed his comeback run in the past two seasons.
runners.comment: Won the Bold Lad off 8lb lower last season, off since October, might need this run

runners.name: Keke
runners.spotlight: One of three for yard; six-time winner, the last three of which have come at the Curragh; testing ground a valid excuse for poor run at that venue on seasonal return but remains 4lb above last win and may need more help from the handicapper.
runners.comment: Six-time winner, ground an excuse for below par Curragh run on return, 4lb above last win

runners.name: Billie Be Quick
runners.spotlight: Won her third handicap off 77 at Chepstow last August (5f, gd-sft); hit with an 11lb hike for an Ayr Listed sixth in September; no impact in Listed races here and at Bath the last twice and on a tough mark returned to handicap company.
runners.comment: No impact in listed company, returns to handicaps off what looks a tough mark

runners.name: Headmaster
runners.spotlight: One of three for yard; won two for William Haggas, a 7f Haydock maiden (good) and a 7f Newbury handicap (soft) on his final run for that yard in October; bought for 48,000gns later that month and the market can guide on first start for new yard.
runners.comment: Won twice at 7f for William Haggas, bought for 48,000gns, market best guide on Irish debut

runners.name: Go Athletico
runners.spotlight: Bounced back to form from out of the blue to win the Scurry at the Curragh last July; has generally struggled since and well beaten in both starts this season at the Curragh; needs a revival.
runners.comment: Won the Scurry last year, generally out of form since, needs a revival

runners.name: Nezeeh
runners.spotlight: Five-time winner at 5f-6f on good and soft ground; is 2lb lower than when winning at Bellewstown in October but has struggled badly in both starts this season; others arrive in better form.
runners.comment: Five-time winner at 5f-6f, 2lb below last win, out of form this season, others preferred

runners.name: City House
runners.spotlight: Smart turf form for George Scott in Bahrain early last year; best run for Ado McGuinness over 6f at Dundalk on penultimate but only beat two rivals home at Newcastle on latest; potentially well handicapped on return to turf and not dismissed.
runners.comment: Smart turf form last year, below best on AW latest but not dismissed back on turf

races.race_name: Irish EBF Median Sires Series Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)

runners.name: Force Noir
runners.spotlight: A March foal and cost 130,000euros as a yearling; beaten a head at the Curragh on opening day of the season, rallying late on; Amo Racing had won that race with smart types in the previous three seasons, and should be hard to beat here.
runners.comment: Sent off 5-6 fav when narrowly defeated in Curragh maiden; should be hard to beat here

runners.name: Bit Of A Buzz
runners.spotlight: Cost 25,000euros as a yearling; second foal; dam seven-race maiden (placed 1m1f in France), half-sister to winners Azmeel (1m2f Group 3), Baisse (German 1m Listed, dam of smart 7f-1m2f winner Best Of Days) and Apache Storm (6f/7f inc 2yo, dam of 1m2f Group 3 winner Galen).
runners.comment: Cost 25,000euros as a yearling and plenty of stakes performers in pedigree

runners.name: No Time Faults
runners.spotlight: Made 20,000gns as a yearling; fourth foal; dam 6f 2yo winner (inc Listed; RPR 97), half-sister to useful 7f/1m winner Caelis, out of maiden half-sister to 1m2f Group 2 winner Fracas and useful 7f/1m winner Tregaron.
runners.comment: Made 20,000gns as a yearling; fourth foal; dam 6f 2yo winner (inc Listed; RPR 97)

runners.name: Lady Patrona
runners.spotlight: Made 11,000gns as a yearling; fifth foal; half-sister to winners Loch Tay (7.5f 2yo/HK 1m dirt; RPR 91), Circo Massimo (French/Belgian 1m1f-11.5f inc AW; 81), Aquiliae (French 1m4f) and Palace Party (German 7.7f); dam unraced sister to 1m 2yo Group 1 winner Proportional
runners.comment: Made 11,000gns as a yearling; fifth foal and all four siblings have won
runners.owner: Miss Kate Harrington & Mrs John Harrington & B Phelan

runners.name: Mr Montauk
runners.spotlight: Seventh foal; brother to winners Sergeant Wilko (5f/6f inc 2yo/AW; RPR 101) and Jungle Monarch (Qatari 6f), half-brother to three winners inc Coul Queen (7f inc 2yo; 95) and Stately Home (1m inc AW; 88); dam well held 7f-9.4f (49); yard's first 2yo runner won lately.
runners.comment: Brother to Sergeant Wilko (5f/6f winner inc 2yo); yard's first 2yo runner won lately

runners.name: The Scallionator
runners.spotlight: Was 10,000euros yearling; fifth foal; half-brother to 6f 2yo/7f winner McTenett (RPR 91); dam 7f winner (81), half-sister to Listed winners Shamshon (5f 2yo) and Boerhan (1m), out of 7f/1m winning half-sister to 6f 2yo Group 1 winner Millisle.
runners.comment: Was 10,000euros yearling; half-brother to 6f 2yo/7f winner McTenett (RPR 91)

runners.name: Whammy
runners.spotlight: Kodi Bear filly showed early pace before weakening when well-beaten fourth of 5 in AW maiden.
runners.comment: Kodi Bear filly showed early pace before weakening when well-beaten in AW maiden

races.race_name: Anglesey Lodge Equine Hospital Woodlands Stakes (Listed Race)

runners.name: Bucanero Fuerte
runners.spotlight: Top class 2yo returned from aborted stud career last year and won a C&D Listed race (good) and a Curragh Group 3 (6f, good); fine third in the Group 1 Flying Five before a below-par run at the Breeders' Cup; soft ground not ideal for seasonal reappearance and better expected now; chance.
runners.comment: Some smart form last year inc' listed C&D win, ground excuse latest, better expected now
runners.owner: AMO Racing Limited & Giselle De Aguiar

runners.name: Mission Central
runners.spotlight: 3yo won three of six starts as a juvenile, including a Group 3 at the Curragh (6f, gd-yld) and a valuable conditions race at Ascot (6f, good); struggled when dropped to 5f at the Breeders' Cup, extending his record to 0-3 at the minimum trip; entitled to be thereabouts on ratings, but others make more appeal.
runners.comment: Won three of six starts as a 2yo inc' a Group 3 but is 0-3 over 5f, others preferred

runners.name: Fahrenheit Seven
runners.spotlight: British raider arrives in terrific form having won back-to-back 5f AW handicaps at Newcastle off 92 and Southwell off 99; effective on good ground; probably needs to improve again on first run in Listed company, but can't be discounted.
runners.comment: Back-to-back AW handicap wins last twice, acts on good ground, up in class, not discounted

runners.name: King Cuan
runners.spotlight: One of two for yard; has won four times at 6f-7f and is 2-2 at this track; sole win last year came in a three-runner auction race at Fairyhouse (6f, good); subsequently placed in Listed and Group 3 company; had a few of these behind when a 3.5l third at Cork (6f, soft) on seasonal return; sharper now but has never won over 5f and Billy Lee prefers Bonus Time.
runners.comment: Best form at 6f-7f, is 2-2 at this venue, had a few of these behind at Cork, sharper now

runners.name: Getreadytorumble
runners.spotlight: British raider from a yard that has started the season strongly; highly progressive last year, winning a maiden and two handicaps before a close third in the Listed Rous Stakes at Ascot (5f, soft); ground-versatile and, while he has a bit to find on paper, can't be dismissed.
runners.comment: Progressive last year, ground versatile, yard in fine fettle, a bit to find, can't dismiss

runners.name: Unique Journey
runners.spotlight: Has won three handicaps at 6f, including one here; not disgraced when fourth in a 6f Cork Listed race on latest, 2.25l behind King Cuan; needs to find a bit more dropped back in trip in the first-time blinkers.
runners.comment: Course winner at 6f, held by King Cuan on latest at Cork, blinkers need to eke out more
runners.owner: N W T Dalzell/James Morgan/Karl Kirwan/Mrs John Murphy

runners.name: Bonus Time
runners.spotlight: One of two for yard; two 6f wins last year, making all to score in a Curragh maiden (good) and a conditions race here (gd-yld); disappointed upped to 7f when last seen at York and loads to find on the figures on her first try at 5f, but interesting that Billy Lee seemingly prefers her to King Cuan.
runners.comment: Course winner at 6f, loads to find on the figures but preferred by Billy Lee

runners.name: Big Gossey
runners.spotlight: Marvelous veteran showed he retained all his ability when winning for the eleventh time in the Listed Gladness Stakes at the Curragh (7f, sft-hvy) a month ago; however, has come up short in the last three renewals of this race and 5f probably sharp enough for him these days.
runners.comment: Wonderful veteran as good as ever last time, this trip may be on sharp side these days

runners.name: Thunderbear
runners.spotlight: Five-time winner best with a bit of cut in the ground; sole win last season came in a Listed heat at this venue (6f, yielding); may have needed his comeback at Cork and likes it here, but would probably like to see some rain.
runners.comment: Five-time winner, won 6f listed here last year on yielding, rain would enhance chance

runners.name: Cardiff By The Sea
runners.spotlight: 3yo filly whose best effort in three runs as a 2yo came when a 1.75l fourth of 23 in the Queen Mary at Royal Ascot; last of seven in a C&D Listed race in July and not seen again until landing a 5f maiden at Cork (soft) 23 days ago; gets all the allowances and that maiden form was boosted at Navan on Saturday.
runners.comment: Fourth in Queen Mary at two, won 5f maiden at Cork latest, form boosted at Navan on Sat'
runners.owner: P J O'Brien, Mrs P Shanahan & M O'Flynn

runners.name: Gazelle d'Or
runners.spotlight: Five-time winner at 5f-6f on good and yielding ground; well beaten on seasonal return at the Curragh (6f, soft) but may have needed that; likely to be ridden to try and nick some black type and could outrun her likely odds if they go a strong gallop.
runners.comment: Five-time winner at 5f-6f, may have needed latest, could outrun likely odds

runners.name: Kerdos
runners.spotlight: Classy sprinter for Clive Cox and won a Listed and a Group 2 for that yard; decent run on debut for this stable at Dundalk in February, but behind a few of these when tailed off at Cork last time; needs to leave that well behind.
runners.comment: Gr' 2 and listed winner for Clive Cox, tailed off Cork latest, needs to leave that behind
runners.owner: Michael Scaife/Liam Ferguson/Simon Murphy/H S Khunkhuna

runners.name: Likedbymike
runners.spotlight: 1-12 on turf but has had a few near misses, including when beaten a short head in a 5f Curragh handicap (soft) last time; ran well over C&D (yielding) last October, but rated 84 and looks to be up against it in this company.
runners.comment: 1-12 on turf, narrowly denied in Curragh h'cap latest, rated 84, up against it
runners.owner: Mrs Michael J Byrne & Jerry P Murphy

runners.name: Bodhi Bear
runners.spotlight: Gelded in the off-season and won on seasonal return for the second year running when landing a Curragh handicap (5f, sft-hvy); this is harder and has lots to find on the figures, but he's relatively lightly raced and there could be more to come.
runners.comment: Won a Curragh handicap on seasonal return, this harder but could be more to come

runners.name: Erosandpsyche
runners.spotlight: Capable of smart form on his day but hasn't won since August 2022 and was well held behind Bodhi Bear on his seasonal reappearance; hopes pinned on cheekpieces sparking a revival.
runners.comment: On long losing run, well held on seasonal return, cheekpieces need to revive

runners.name: Moss Tucker
runners.spotlight: Former Flying Five winner landed this race in 2023 and 2024; lost form in the second half of 2024 and missed all of last season; entitled to need this run after such a long layoff.
runners.comment: Group 1 winner, won this in 2023 and 2024, missed all last season, may need this run

races.race_name: Naas Racecourse Business Club Blackwater Handicap (Premier Handicap)

runners.name: Bobby McGee
runners.spotlight: Ran nice races in early summer at two; back from lengthy layoff, and having been gelded, to win Gowran maiden (7f, soft); first go at a mile.
runners.comment: Back from lengthy layoff, and having been gelded, to win Gowran maiden (7f, soft)

runners.name: Invincible Will
runners.spotlight: Solid nursery performer on easy ground, winning over 7f at the Curragh; good effort on return when second at Cork (1m, soft to heavy).
runners.comment: Solid nursery performer on easy ground, good effort on return at Cork (1m, soft to heavy)

runners.name: Jamestown
runners.spotlight: Clear of the rest when chasing home Bobby McGee at Gowran  (7f, soft); handed mark of 81 for handicap debut.
runners.comment: Clear of the rest when chasing home Bobby McGee at Gowran (7f, soft); handicap debut

runners.name: Controlled
runners.spotlight: Ex-Ballydoyle performer won 5f maiden at this track last term and fine second in Madrid Handicap over 7f here on his return; well beaten last time and vulnerable off this mark; best form on soft.
runners.comment: Fine second in Madrid Handicap over 7f here on his return; well beaten last time
runners.owner: W D Gannon & Paul M O'Loughlin

runners.name: Trojan Warrior
runners.spotlight: Beat a smart rival in 6f Curragh maiden (heavy) in October; only 6-1 for 7f Guineas trial at Leopardstown, still looked green and didn't land a blow; could be a different proposition in a handicap with that run behind him and headgear added.
runners.comment: Didn't land a blow in 7f Guineas trial, but could be a different proposition in a handicap
runners.owner: Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier

runners.name: Son Of Beauty
runners.spotlight: AW maiden winner over 1m in early March; later that month was beaten around 5l in Madrid Handicap here over 7f; better ground this time will help.
runners.comment: Beaten around 5l in Madrid Handicap here over 7f; better ground this time will help.

runners.name: Felix Guido
runners.spotlight: Unbeaten in three starts in Poland at up to 6.5f, making all each time; impossible to assess but respect for the trainer means he can't be taken lightly; market useful.
runners.comment: Unbeaten in three starts in Poland at up to 6.5f, making all each time; hard to assess

runners.name: Alphecca
runners.spotlight: Progressive on easy ground in the autumn; off the mark over this trip in early October and won valuable restricted contest over 7f here just six days later; yard, and owner, have won two of the last three renewals.
runners.comment: Progressive on easy ground in the autumn; won valuable restricted contest over 7f here

runners.name: Manton Bay
runners.spotlight: Shaped well over 6f on debut and then won AW maiden over 1m in November; looks yard's second-string on jockey arrangements; seasonal and handicap debut.
runners.comment: Won AW maiden over 1m in November; seasonal and handicap bow; looks yard's second-string

runners.name: How'sthebai
runners.spotlight: Off the mark in 1m AW maiden in October on last of three juvenile outings; seasonal and handicap debut; 1lb wrong.
runners.comment: Won AW maiden over 1m in October on last of three juvenile outings; handicap debut

races.race_name: Race & Stay Fillies Maiden

runners.name: Cape Primrose
runners.spotlight: Well-bred filly is rated 79 after three starts; just under 6l ahead of Arrietty when runner-up at Thurles on second start last year; soft ground may not have suited when disappointing on return at Gowran; should go well in first-time cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Rated 79 after three starts, ground may not have suited latest, cheekpieces on, go well
runners.owner: Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor

runners.name: Arrietty
runners.spotlight: Rated 77; runner-up in three of four starts on AW including at this trip the last twice at Dundalk earlier this year; well held by Cape Primrose on sole turf start at Thurles on debut but that rival had the benefit of a previous run; in the mix if ready after break.
runners.comment: Rated 77, runner up in three of four starts on AW, in the mix if ready after 66 day break

runners.name: Glyfada
runners.spotlight: Beaten 0.5l on debut by a more experienced rival at the Curragh (1m1f) on very testing ground when a 4-1 shot; pedigree suggests this ground and step up in trip will suit and she is a leading contender.
runners.comment: Fine effort on debut, better ground and longer trip should suit here, leading chance

runners.name: Sounds Great
runners.spotlight: Identical RPRs (75) when fourth on debut at Galway (9.5f, sft-hvy) last October and when a 1.5l second of 7 on seasonal return at Gowran (9.5f, soft); place claims if acting on this sounder surface.
runners.comment: Identical RPR of 75 for both starts, 1.5l runner up at Gowran latest, place claims

runners.name: French Blond
runners.spotlight: Almanzor filly; E62,000 yearling; dam 5.7f-1m winner (inc 2yo Group 3; RPR 106), half-sister to winners Keeper's Heart (Listed-placed 1m 2yo), Elite Legion (7f 2yo) and Muntahez (1m1f), out of Listed-placed 6f 2yo winner; nicely-bred newcomer worth a market check.
runners.comment: Almanzor filly, dam 2yo Group 3 winner, nicely bred newcomer, check the market

runners.name: Soigne
runners.spotlight: Ghaiyyath filly; E52,000 yearling; half-sister to winners Conviction (7f AW 2yo; RPR 76), Angelica K (1m AW; 65) and Lord Howe (Italian 1m/8.7f inc AW); dam 8.5f AW winner, sister to 1m Group 1 winner Timepiece, half-sister to 1m2f 2yo Group 1 winner Passage Of Time; preferred by Gary Carroll; check the market.
runners.comment: Ghaiyyath filly, dam 8.5f AW winner, preferred by Gary Carroll, check market

runners.name: Shambata
runners.spotlight: New Bay filly; dam 7f winner (RPR 87), half-sister to winners Shembala (French 1m6f Listed) and Sheberghan (10.5f), out of Group-placed 7f winner; debutante from in form yard needs checking in the market.
runners.comment: New Bay filly, dam a 7f winner, newcomer from in form yard needs checking in market
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late H H Aga Khan

runners.name: Cite Florale
runners.spotlight: 33-1 for her debut at Dundalk (1m) in November and not disgraced; moves up in distance for her turf debut and is probably best watched unless the market speaks.
runners.comment: Not disgraced on debut at Dundalk in Nov' when 33-1, upped in trip, check the market

runners.name: Charm Of Venice
runners.spotlight: Showed nothing on debut at Leopardstown in October (7f, yielding); better effort at Dundalk (1m) in January when a 3.25l fifth of 14; moves up in trip with Keane a notable booking, but more needed.
runners.comment: Improved on second start at Dundalk, moves up in trip with Keane up, needs more

runners.name: De Songbird
runners.spotlight: Sent off at 200-1 when finishing in mid-field on debut at Leopardstown 12 days ago (1m, yld-sft); should improve for that initial experience but lots more needed up in trip.
runners.comment: 200-1 when midfield on debut at Leopardstown, should improve, more needed up in trip

races.race_name: Download The Raceday Ready App Amateur Jockeys' Handicap

runners.name: Alazwar
runners.spotlight: Has gone close when placed twice this year, latest when second of 11 at Lingfield (1m, AW) last Monday; on a decent mark and is one to consider.
runners.comment: Has gone close when placed twice this year, latest when second at Lingfield last Monday.

runners.name: Rainwater
runners.spotlight: 2-34 but only faded late after racing prominently at Doncaster (1m, good to soft) and met trouble in running when fifth of 12 at Southwell (7f, AW; 100-30) 12 days ago; on career-low mark and may be ready to take advantage.
runners.comment: Met trouble in running when fifth at Southwell 12 days ago; career-low mark now

runners.name: Book Of Life
runners.spotlight: Doesn't win very often (2-38) but he's made the frame on his three starts this month and should not be far away.
runners.comment: Has made the frame on his three starts this month and should not be far away.

runners.name: Pessoa
runners.spotlight: All seven wins have been here, including three over C&D; in the money twice here since his last win and holds place claims at least.
runners.comment: All seven wins have been here, including three over C&D.

runners.name: No Knee Never
runners.spotlight: Rarely runs a bad race and won for the third time this year under Henry Callan at Lingfield (1m, AW) last Monday; possibly found the run coming too soon when below best back on turf at Beverley three days later; should not be far away if another quick run doesn't count against him.
runners.comment: Won at Lingfield last Monday; down the field at Beverley three days later.

runners.name: Sassy Glory
runners.spotlight: Breakthrough win at Newcastle (1m, AW) last September was her 23rd career start; not so hot since, so it's probably best to look elsewhere for win purposes.
runners.comment: Won at Newcastle last September on her 23rd career start; not so hot since.
runners.owner: Ursa Major & Mark Walford Racing Club

runners.name: Volendam
runners.spotlight: Placed on four of her last five AW starts before getting off the mark at the 18th attempt back on turf at Bath (1m, good) 22 days ago; back up 7lb but she should remain competitive without looking an obvious one to follow up.
runners.comment: Off the mark at the 18th attempt on the return to turf at Bath 22 days ago; back up 7lb

runners.name: Always Fearless
runners.spotlight: Has an inconsistent profile, having scored over C&D last month off a 2lb lower mark but has only beaten two home in three runs since; hard to assess.
runners.comment: Has an inconsistent profile and has run poorly three times since a C&D win last month.

runners.name: Wyvern
runners.spotlight: All six wins have been on AW but has yet to win beyond 1m and not in the best of form at present; has dropped to a career-low mark but doesn't look ready to take advantage.
runners.comment: All six wins have been on AW but yet to win beyond 1m; on a career-low mark now

runners.name: King Of The Dance
runners.spotlight: Has run with credit since winning at Chepstow (7f, good to firm) last July but is a victim of his own consistency and probably needs to drop a couple of pounds before finding another opening.
runners.comment: Has run with credit since his win at Chepstow last July but looks weighted to his best

runners.name: Molly Valentine
runners.spotlight: All four wins when trained by Dean Ivory; finished last of 12 at Lingfield (1m2f, AW) on her sole start for Clare Hobson last month; headgear removed for stable debut; hard to make a case for.
runners.comment: Finished last at Lingfield on her sole start for Clare Hobson last month; another new yard

runners.name: Port Louis
runners.spotlight: Both wins for Noel Meade in Ireland; runner-up in a big-field amateur jockeys' handicap at Doncaster (1m, good to soft) last month; slow to start last time at Lingfield and has an inconsistent profile.
runners.comment: Slow to start last time at Lingfield and has an inconsistent profile.

runners.name: Stockpyle
runners.spotlight: Four wins for William Muir and Chris Grassick; winless for current yard last year and has dropped to a career-low mark on first run for 209 days; may be best watched this time.
runners.comment: Winless for current yard; has dropped to a career-low mark but may be best watched.

races.race_name: Enjoy Summer Racing At Wolverhampton Racecourse Maiden Fillies' Stakes (GBB/GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Littlecote
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in a couple of similar events at Southwell and Kempton (1m4f and 1m3f) this year and this looks to be a good opportunity to get off the mark.
runners.comment: Runner-up in a couple of similar events at Southwell and Kempton this year; obvious claims

runners.name: Shayhana
runners.spotlight: Second of three on her debut at Lingfield (1m4f, AW) last November; can be expected to show improvement for her good yard; holds claims.
runners.comment: Second on her debut at Lingfield last November; can be expected to show improvement

runners.name: Exquisite Skye
runners.spotlight: Placed three times for Alice Haynes last year; steps up in trip on stable debut and more required.
runners.comment: Placed three times for Alice Haynes last year; steps up in trip on stable debut.

runners.name: Electric Arc
runners.spotlight: Third foal; by Postponed; half-sister to 7f 2yo/1m4f Listed winner Novakai (RPR 109); dam 1m4f-1m6f winner (including AW; 98), half-sister to 1m2f Listed winner Elas Ruby, out of Listed-placed 1m 2yo winning half-sister to 2m2f Group 2 winner Pallasator; has a good middle-distance pedigree and yard respected; needs a look in the betting.
runners.comment: Has a good middle-distance pedigree; respected yard and worth a look in the betting
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: Noble Grace
runners.spotlight: Beaten a neck in a bumper on her racecourse debut but a disappointing maiden over hurdles; tall order on her belated Flat debut.
runners.comment: Runner-up in a bumper but has a tall order on her belated Flat debut.

runners.name: Semele
runners.spotlight: 0-3 in three bumpers; fourth of eight on Flat debut at Kempton (1m3f, AW) four weeks ago; needs to show improvement.
runners.comment: Fourth on Flat debut at Kempton four weeks ago; needs to show improvement.
runners.owner: Mr And Mrs A E Pakenham

runners.name: Wonderful Wendy
runners.spotlight: Has shown little in her two starts and needs more time.
runners.comment: Has shown little in her two starts and needs more time.

runners.name: Six Blue
runners.spotlight: Tailed off at big odds on all three bumper starts; performed better when sixth of eight on her Flat debut at Kempton (1m3f, AW) last month but should not be good enough for this race.
runners.comment: Sixth on her Flat debut at Kempton last month but should not be good enough for this race.

races.race_name: Country And Western Raceday - 28th June Handicap

runners.name: Lordsbridge Blu
runners.spotlight: Won twice, including over C&D; most encouraging when runner-up over C&D four weeks ago on his return from a break and should go close here.
runners.comment: Most encouraging when runner-up over C&D four weeks ago.

runners.name: Jez Bomb
runners.spotlight: Four wins last year and has run with credit on his three starts this term, albeit he is 6lb higher than his last winning mark; should run well but needs a career-best to get his head in front.
runners.comment: Four wins last year and has run with credit on his three starts this term.

runners.name: Echalar
runners.spotlight: Won here (7f) off 1lb higher in January when trained by Jane Chapple-Hyam; posted easily his most encouraging display since joining current yard when a keeping-on sixth of 13 at Southwell (1m, AW) 20 days ago; steps up in trip on a career-low mark and must come into consideration.
runners.comment: Best display since joining current yard when sixth at Southwell 20 days ago.

runners.name: Epictetus
runners.spotlight: Has gone close recently and was only beaten half a length at Lingfield (1m, AW) 13 days ago but is without a win since landing a Group 3 for the Gosdens three years ago; looks sure to be thereabouts once again.
runners.comment: Has gone close recently and was only beaten half a length at Lingfield 13 days ago.

runners.name: Renesmee
runners.spotlight: In good heart at present, winning at Chelmsford (1m, AW) last month off 6lb lower and in the money twice since; steps back up in trip and she should run well again.
runners.comment: Won at Chelmsford last month off 6lb lower and in the money twice since.

runners.name: Newfangled
runners.spotlight: Has two course wins, including one over C&D; fair fifth of 11 at Lingfield (1m, AW) 13 days ago an first outing for six months; should come on for the run but looks high enough in the weights for now.
runners.comment: Fair fifth at Lingfield 13 days ago an first outing for six months.

runners.name: Big Sip
runners.spotlight: Landed a seller over C&D last October; only beaten 2l when sixth of 11 at Lingfield (1m, AW) 13 days ago on his first run back and should be suited by the step up in trip; can go well.
runners.comment: Only beaten 2l when sixth at Lingfield 13 days ago.

runners.name: City Of God
runners.spotlight: Landed seller on final start for Karl Burke at Chelmsford (1m, AW) in February in first-time cheekpieces; headgear absent when fifth of nine at Kempton (1m, AW) three weeks ago on stable debut; cheekpieces back on; stepping up in trip but may need to come down in the weights.
runners.comment: Landed seller for Karl Burke at Chelmsford in February; fifth at Kempton last time.

runners.name: Primo Lara
runners.spotlight: Won twice for Jane Chapple-Hyam in 2024; 0-4 for current yard and has had wind surgery since has last run in October; best watched for now.
runners.comment: 0-4 for current yard and has had wind surgery since has last run in October.

races.race_name: Get Raceday Ready Restricted Maiden Stakes (For Horses In Bands C And D) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Clementines Star
runners.spotlight: Perhaps a shade disappointing on her second 2yo start but her keeping-on third of 12 at Newcastle (6f) was a promising debut; has a big role to play if back at that level on this reappearance outing.
runners.comment: Second 2yo run was a shade disappointing but the debut was promising; considered

runners.name: Imelda
runners.spotlight: Out of a close relative of 7f 2yo Group 3 winner Nations Alexander; ran well from the front when second over C&D on debut three weeks ago; obvious contender if able to build on that performance.
runners.comment: Ran well from the front when second over C&D on debut three weeks ago

runners.name: Vidmiyr
runners.spotlight: Made the frame on the last two of his three 2yo starts for Johnny Murtagh in Ireland, more recently when coming from the rear for fourth at Dundalk (7f) in October; bought for 48,000euros in November; has good chance here if fully tuned up for stable debut.
runners.comment: Made the frame on last two of his three Irish 2yo starts; good chance on the figures
runners.owner: James Paul Services To Industry Ltd

runners.name: Cotai Eye Joe
