runners.spotlight: Fair form when third on both starts, latterly in tactical Newcastle race (7f) last month; probably needs to improve here but that's possible.
runners.comment: Fair form when third on both starts, latterly in tactical Newcastle race (7f) last month
runners.owner: Ursa Major Racing & Alfa Site Services

runners.name: Thai Princess
runners.spotlight: In a top stable but began career with two low-key runs, the latter a sixth of eight over C&D this month (race in which Imelda was second).
runners.comment: With a top stable but began career with two low-key runs, the latter over C&D

runners.name: King Cherry
runners.spotlight: Friendless in the betting but certainly not disgraced when midfield on debut at Kempton (6f) three weeks ago; now needs to build on that effort.
runners.comment: Friendless in the betting but not disgraced when midfield on recent debut at Kempton (6f)

runners.name: Ernie McCrew
runners.spotlight: Fourth foal; half-brother to 6f winner Babe Alicious (including AW; RPR 61); dam placed 1m 2yo (including AW; 68), half-sister to 1m5f Listed winner Charleston Lady and a Japanese 1m Group 3 winner; stable has only occasional debut winners.
runners.comment: Half-brother to a 6f winner; stable has only occasional debut winners

runners.name: Santiago Boy
runners.spotlight: 24,000gns yearling; sixth foal; half-brother to winners Military Mission (9.5f-1m4f including Australian Group 2; RPR 116) and Augeval (Italian 1m); dam German 7f-1m winner; already gelded but makes debut in a run-of-the-mill maiden; market helpful.
runners.comment: Half-brother to an Australian Group 2 winner; makes debut in a run-of-the-mill maiden

runners.name: Difford Girl
runners.spotlight: Well beaten on first two appearances, more recently when 200-1 for C&D maiden this month; hood added today.
runners.comment: Well beaten on first two appearances, more recently when 200-1 for C&D maiden this month

runners.name: Niffler
runners.spotlight: Down the field on first two starts, both at Southwell this month (7f/1m); needs to take a big step forward.
runners.comment: Down the field on first two starts, both at Southwell this month (7f/1m)

runners.name: Funlover
runners.spotlight: First foal from a prolific 1m-1m2f winner; well beaten on debut at Goodwood (6f, good) in October but was only 15-2 that day and the early stages did not go well; ought to be capable of much better.
runners.comment: Well beaten on sole 2yo start but in good hands and ought to be capable of much better

runners.name: Northern Soldier
runners.spotlight: Struggled on both 2yo starts (7.3f/7f) and can only be watched on this reappearance outing.
runners.comment: Struggled on both 2yo starts (7.3f/7f) and can only be watched on this reappearance

races.race_name: Free Digital Racecard At raceday-ready.com Handicap

runners.name: Shes Got The Blues
runners.spotlight: 11-race maiden; lost out only by a short head off basement mark at Southwell (1m, AW) this month but went up 3lb for that, has wide draw here and needs to prove she can cope with the drop back in trip.
runners.comment: Went close at Southwell (1m) this month; drawn wide; needs to prove herself over 7f

runners.name: Giles Glory
runners.spotlight: Emphatic winner in first-time visor for Joseph Parr at Lingfield (7f) in January and signed off for him with two pretty good fourths on Polytrack in February/March; subsequently changed hands for 2,800gns; big player if he responds well to first-time blinkers on stable debut.
runners.comment: Won in first-time visor for Joseph Parr this year; tries in blinkers on stable debut

runners.name: Starakova
runners.spotlight: Unplaced all seven starts; began season with quite promising fifth of 11 at Chelmsford (7f) in March but her turf run a fortnight ago was disappointing and she now has a point to prove.
runners.comment: 0-7; ran quite on well on seasonal debut at Chelmsford but well held on turf since
runners.owner: Howard Bolly Sutton Molony Weller Cowles

runners.name: Sirhana
runners.spotlight: Yet to be placed after six starts but her fifth of 12 at Southwell (7f) last month was quite encouraging; 2lb lower here and capable 5lb claimer Ethan Tindall is enlisted; worth a second look.
runners.comment: Unplaced all six starts but last month's Southwell fifth was quite encouraging

runners.name: Electrocution
runners.spotlight: Placed twice off today's mark since opening account over C&D in February; needs to rebound from a below-par effort this month but still features prominently in calculations.
runners.comment: Didn't fire last time but has been placed twice since his C&D win in February

runners.name: Monk's Hill
runners.spotlight: Bumped into an improver when second at Newcastle (7f) last month and is of interest if judged on that performance but was well beaten on turf a fortnight ago and is now 0-7.
runners.comment: Second to an improver on latest AW start but well beaten on turf since
runners.owner: Alfa Site Services Ltd & Graeme Curnow

runners.name: Resalah
runners.spotlight: Not disgraced when seventh here (8.6f) last month but yet to be placed after nine starts; soundly beaten the only previous time she wore cheekpieces (last season); difficult to enthuse over.
runners.comment: Not disgraced here (8.6f) last month but yet to be placed after nine starts

runners.name: Frankali
runners.spotlight: Lost her chance at the start on latest outing and is now 0-7 but has twice finished third on Polytrack this year and is quite well handicapped on those efforts; could have a say, despite awkward draw.
runners.comment: Blew the start last time; quite well handicapped on this year's two Polytrack thirds

runners.name: Dora Vita
runners.spotlight: Belied 80-1 odds when keeping-on fourth over C&D on last month's handicap debut but was well beaten when upped to 1m at Lingfield a fortnight ago; others have more convincing profiles.
runners.comment: Big-odds fourth over C&D on handicap debut but well beaten at Lingfield since

runners.name: Lc Tiffen
runners.spotlight: Soundly beaten a huge odds in three turf novices races (6f-7.3f) last summer; makes handicap/AW debut after 263-day absence; hood fitted.
runners.comment: Well beaten at huge odds in thee turf novices races (6f-7.3f) last summer; not seen since

runners.name: Clipstone
runners.spotlight: Unexposed after only four starts but absent since finishing well down the field on nursery debut at Bath (5.7f, good to firm) in September; gelded in November; upped in trip for this seasonal/AW debut.
runners.comment: Well down the field on nursery debut (5.7f) in September; up in trip for AW debut

runners.name: Based
runners.spotlight: Well down the field on all three 2yo starts, albeit not beaten that far at Kempton (7f) on latest outing in November (gelded soon afterwards); improvement required on this seasonal/handicap debut.
runners.comment: Well down the field on all three 2yo starts; improvement required on handicap debut

races.race_name: Hotel And Conferencing At Wolverhampton Racecourse Handicap

runners.name: Crown Inn To Win
runners.spotlight: Progressive form since sent into handicaps this year, set a lot to do when a clear second of six to Forever Perfect over C&D six days ago; hood retained and tongue tied is also added; player off an unchanged mark.
runners.comment: Clear second over C&D six days ago; tongue tie added; player off an unchanged mark

runners.name: Montu
runners.spotlight: Arrives in good nick, cheekpieces added when third of 11 in 1m4f Kempton handicap four weeks ago; he can go well again off the same mark in his bid for a breakthrough victory.
runners.comment: Arrives in good nick, third at Kempton latest; can go well in bid for maiden win

runners.name: Just Call Me Mo
runners.spotlight: Yet to offer much in five outings, a never-dangerous sixth in 9.5f handicap here 17 days ago; cheekpieces go on now.
runners.comment: Yet to offer much in five outings, never-dangerous sixth in 9.5f handicap here 17 days ago

runners.name: Bradbury
runners.spotlight: Sent off 9-4 for his handicap debut at Lingfield (1m2f) last month but he went keenly before fading into fifth; tongue strap goes on now with more needed if he's to get off the mark over this longer trip.
runners.comment: Keen before a fading fifth at Lingfield last time; more needed if he's to get off the mark

runners.name: Song Of The Stars
runners.spotlight: Ghaiyyath filly who advanced her form on her handicap debut when fourth of seven over C&D last month; she needs to take another step forward now though.
runners.comment: Good fourth of seven over C&D last month; needs to take another step forward now though

runners.name: Favourite Friend
runners.spotlight: Sea The Moon gelding; always behind on his four starts to date, coming in a well-held sixth of eight in 9.5f maiden here on his return 18 days ago; much more is expected now he goes handicapping stepped up in trip; interesting.
runners.comment: Always behind in 4 runs; much more expected now up in trip for handicap debut; interesting

runners.name: Maryland Star
runners.spotlight: Returned from six months off with her best effort when third of eight in 9.5f maiden here 18 days ago; may do better still now venturing into handicaps.
runners.comment: Best effort when third here 18 days ago; may do better still now venturing into handicaps

runners.name: Forever Perfect
runners.spotlight: Much improved recently for the step up to 1m4f and she got off the mark over C&D six days ago, edging out Crown Inn To Win late on; not taken lightly under a 6lb penalty.
runners.comment: Got off the mark over C&D six days ago; she's not taken lightly under a 6lb penalty

runners.name: Maxwellcan
runners.spotlight: Failed to kick on for Charlie Johnston and he was gelded before coming in last of five in 1m4f Kempton handicap in January; starts out for a new yard with something to prove; first-time tongue tie.
runners.comment: Last of five in Kempton h'cap in January; starts for new yard here with something to prove

runners.name: Head Girl
runners.spotlight: Cut little ice in a trio of novices (around 1m) last autumn; needs this switch to handicaps/longer trip to spark major improvement.
runners.comment: Cut little ice in trio of novices last autumn; needs switch to h'cap to spark improvement

runners.name: Ada Rose
runners.spotlight: Has offered little in her three runs to date, beating just one in 7f novice here in January; handicap debutante.
runners.comment: Little show in three runs, beating one in 7f novice here in January; handicap debutante

races.race_name: Get The Inside Track With raceday-ready.com Handicap

runners.name: Forever Noah
runners.spotlight: Successful in a 6f handicap at Chelmsford in February off a 4lb lower mark; runner-up here (6f) on his last two starts and he has run well over 5f in the past; should give his running.
runners.comment: Runner-up over 6f here on his last two starts; has run well over 5f in the past; contender

runners.name: Henery Hawk
runners.spotlight: C&D winner; two good runs here last month but he was never in the hunt back here 18 days ago; has the ability to feature but he will need luck in running from stall 1 given his racing style.
runners.comment: Conditions to suit and in the money over C&D twice last month; not so good last time

runners.name: Macarone
runners.spotlight: Won two 6f handicaps here last autumn and he is now 4lb lower than for the latter win; more recent efforts leave him with a point to prove but the fitting of a tongue-tie could give him a lift and Silvestre De Sousa is a positive booking.
runners.comment: Well handicapped on autumn form; tongue-tie could help; revival not out of the question

runners.name: Candy Warhol
runners.spotlight: On his last winning mark and there have been several good runs since he had a wind operation last October, including last week's Lingfield second (5f AW); drawn widest here though and that may prove his undoing.
runners.comment: Arrives on the back of a good run at Lingfield; widest stall could prove troublesome
runners.owner: Homecroft Wealth, Paul J Dixon & Partners

runners.name: Street Life
runners.spotlight: Won twice on AW this winter, including C&D, and he is now 2lb lower than for the second win; has gone quiet more recently though and could prove vulnerable for win purposes once again.
runners.comment: Conditions fine and 2lb lower than for Southwell win in January; gone quiet more recently

runners.name: Isla Bella
runners.spotlight: Losing run stands at 18 but she has run some good races in defeat for Joey Ramsden, including over C&D; down the field at Doncaster on Friday but she's capable of bouncing back quickly; stable also runs Forever Noah.
runners.comment: On a losing run but fully effective over C&D; below par on turf on Friday; can bounce back

runners.name: Master Dandy
runners.spotlight: Ran poorly at Newcastle last month but he had been in good form beforehand, winning over C&D and at Chelmsford in January; holds Forever Noah on the Chelmsford run and could bounce back after a short break.
runners.comment: Poor run latest; thriving beforehand, including a C&D win; short break may revive fortunes

runners.name: Basholo
runners.spotlight: Won twice over C&D in late 2025, the latter success coming off a 2lb higher mark; more recent efforts need bettering but this is likely to be run to suit and dropping back into a 0-60 could see her bounce back to form.
runners.comment: Two C&D wins in late 2025; dangerous mark and now drops back into 0-60 company

runners.name: Dark Side Prince
runners.spotlight: Prolific 5f winner, including over C&D, most recently at Chelmsford in October off 2lb lower; however, he was disappointing back at Chelmsford in February and has a bit to prove this time.
runners.comment: Prolific winner but down the field on his return in February; has a bit to prove this time

races.race_name: CARBONETZERO MAIDEN STAKES
Anglesey Lodge Equine Hospital Woodlands Stakes (Listed Race)

runners.name: Bucanero Fuerte
runners.spotlight: Top class 2yo returned from aborted stud career last year and won a C&D Listed race (good) and a Curragh Group 3 (6f, good); fine third in the Group 1 Flying Five before a below-par run at the Breeders' Cup; soft ground not ideal for seasonal reappearance and better expected now; chance.
runners.comment: Some smart form last year inc' listed C&D win, ground excuse latest, better expected now
runners.owner: AMO Racing Limited & Giselle De Aguiar

runners.name: Mission Central
runners.spotlight: 3yo won three of six starts as a juvenile, including a Group 3 at the Curragh (6f, gd-yld) and a valuable conditions race at Ascot (6f, good); struggled when dropped to 5f at the Breeders' Cup, extending his record to 0-3 at the minimum trip; entitled to be thereabouts on ratings, but others make more appeal.
runners.comment: Won three of six starts as a 2yo inc' a Group 3 but is 0-3 over 5f, others preferred

runners.name: Fahrenheit Seven
runners.spotlight: British raider arrives in terrific form having won back-to-back 5f AW handicaps at Newcastle off 92 and Southwell off 99; effective on good ground; probably needs to improve again on first run in Listed company, but can't be discounted.
runners.comment: Back-to-back AW handicap wins last twice, acts on good ground, up in class, not discounted

runners.name: King Cuan
runners.spotlight: One of two for yard; has won four times at 6f-7f and is 2-2 at this track; sole win last year came in a three-runner auction race at Fairyhouse (6f, good); subsequently placed in Listed and Group 3 company; had a few of these behind when a 3.5l third at Cork (6f, soft) on seasonal return; sharper now but has never won over 5f and Billy Lee prefers Bonus Time.
runners.comment: Best form at 6f-7f, is 2-2 at this venue, had a few of these behind at Cork, sharper now

runners.name: Getreadytorumble
runners.spotlight: British raider from a yard that has started the season strongly; highly progressive last year, winning a maiden and two handicaps before a close third in the Listed Rous Stakes at Ascot (5f, soft); ground-versatile and, while he has a bit to find on paper, can't be dismissed.
runners.comment: Progressive last year, ground versatile, yard in fine fettle, a bit to find, can't dismiss

runners.name: Unique Journey
runners.spotlight: Has won three handicaps at 6f, including one here; not disgraced when fourth in a 6f Cork Listed race on latest, 2.25l behind King Cuan; needs to find a bit more dropped back in trip in the first-time blinkers.
runners.comment: Course winner at 6f, held by King Cuan on latest at Cork, blinkers need to eke out more
runners.owner: N W T Dalzell/James Morgan/Karl Kirwan/Mrs John Murphy

runners.name: Bonus Time
runners.spotlight: One of two for yard; two 6f wins last year, making all to score in a Curragh maiden (good) and a conditions race here (gd-yld); disappointed upped to 7f when last seen at York and loads to find on the figures on her first try at 5f, but interesting that Billy Lee seemingly prefers her to King Cuan.
runners.comment: Course winner at 6f, loads to find on the figures but preferred by Billy Lee

runners.name: Big Gossey
runners.spotlight: Marvelous veteran showed he retained all his ability when winning for the eleventh time in the Listed Gladness Stakes at the Curragh (7f, sft-hvy) a month ago; however, has come up short in the last three renewals of this race and 5f probably sharp enough for him these days.
runners.comment: Wonderful veteran as good as ever last time, this trip may be on sharp side these days

runners.name: Thunderbear
runners.spotlight: Five-time winner best with a bit of cut in the ground; sole win last season came in a Listed heat at this venue (6f, yielding); may have needed his comeback at Cork and likes it here, but would probably like to see some rain.
runners.comment: Five-time winner, won 6f listed here last year on yielding, rain would enhance chance

runners.name: Cardiff By The Sea
runners.spotlight: 3yo filly whose best effort in three runs as a 2yo came when a 1.75l fourth of 23 in the Queen Mary at Royal Ascot; last of seven in a C&D Listed race in July and not seen again until landing a 5f maiden at Cork (soft) 23 days ago; gets all the allowances and that maiden form was boosted at Navan on Saturday.
runners.comment: Fourth in Queen Mary at two, won 5f maiden at Cork latest, form boosted at Navan on Sat'
runners.owner: P J O'Brien, Mrs P Shanahan & M O'Flynn

runners.name: Gazelle d'Or
runners.spotlight: Five-time winner at 5f-6f on good and yielding ground; well beaten on seasonal return at the Curragh (6f, soft) but may have needed that; likely to be ridden to try and nick some black type and could outrun her likely odds if they go a strong gallop.
runners.comment: Five-time winner at 5f-6f, may have needed latest, could outrun likely odds

runners.name: Kerdos
runners.spotlight: Classy sprinter for Clive Cox and won a Listed and a Group 2 for that yard; decent run on debut for this stable at Dundalk in February, but behind a few of these when tailed off at Cork last time; needs to leave that well behind.
runners.comment: Gr' 2 and listed winner for Clive Cox, tailed off Cork latest, needs to leave that behind
runners.owner: Michael Scaife/Liam Ferguson/Simon Murphy/H S Khunkhuna

runners.name: Likedbymike
runners.spotlight: 1-12 on turf but has had a few near misses, including when beaten a short head in a 5f Curragh handicap (soft) last time; ran well over C&D (yielding) last October, but rated 84 and looks to be up against it in this company.
runners.comment: 1-12 on turf, narrowly denied in Curragh h'cap latest, rated 84, up against it
runners.owner: Mrs Michael J Byrne & Jerry P Murphy

runners.name: Bodhi Bear
runners.spotlight: Gelded in the off-season and won on seasonal return for the second year running when landing a Curragh handicap (5f, sft-hvy); this is harder and has lots to find on the figures, but he's relatively lightly raced and there could be more to come.
runners.comment: Won a Curragh handicap on seasonal return, this harder but could be more to come

runners.name: Erosandpsyche
runners.spotlight: Capable of smart form on his day but hasn't won since August 2022 and was well held behind Bodhi Bear on his seasonal reappearance; hopes pinned on cheekpieces sparking a revival.
runners.comment: On long losing run, well held on seasonal return, cheekpieces need to revive

runners.name: Moss Tucker
runners.spotlight: Former Flying Five winner landed this race in 2023 and 2024; lost form in the second half of 2024 and missed all of last season; entitled to need this run after such a long layoff.
runners.comment: Group 1 winner, won this in 2023 and 2024, missed all last season, may need this run
